architecture_fit.note: Reaches ~0.997 optimality with feasible_rate 1.0, but the mechanism is an iterative best-improvement local search loosely framed as top-1 attention — not a transformer delta from base_model.py.

baseline_comparison.note: Greedy ratio baseline measured on identical batches and beaten at all 5 capacity fracs; the win is consistent though the margin is small (+0.005 canonical lift).

faithfulness.note: Hand-built circuit with no learned model; causal-by-construction and ablations (drop feasibility mask / delta>0 gate) are described but not executed/shown.

operating_range.note: Stable and above baseline across the full canonical capacity sweep 0.3–0.7 with feasible 1.0, but no exploration beyond the mandated range or to breaking points.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Entirely hand-set circuit with no learned weights that reproduces near-optimal knapsack behaviour — exactly what this bonus rewards.

visual_judgement.note: Clean two-panel Demo: optimality-vs-capacity with circuit/greedy/optimum reference lines plus lift bars annotated with feasibility — claim is legible.

visualisation_rationale.note: README justifies y-axis (1−gap), x-axis (capacity_frac), the greedy baseline and DP-optimum references, and the feasibility annotation, tying each to the goal's two sub-questions.

notes: A solid hand-built attempt that fixes the prior pass's two failures: it starts from the greedy selection and applies only feasibility-preserving, value-increasing swaps, guaranteeing it never falls below greedy while holding feasible_rate=1.0 and beating the baseline at every capacity. Its weaknesses are that the 'attention' framing is a thin dressing over a standard local-search heuristic (no base_model.py delta), the lift over greedy is small, and the proposed ablations are only described rather than run. Visualisation and rationale are strong, putting mean rubric score at ~4.0.
